html:
Fist Full of Jam is a Fruit Food Item cooked in a Crock Pot by adding any Fruits except Dragon Fruit, which will lead to a Dragonpie instead. However, adding more than 2.5 units of Fruits may result in Fruit Medley instead. It takes 10 seconds to cook.

No Meats, Vegetables, or Twigs should be added, otherwise, it will result in Meatballs, Ratatouille, or Wet Goop respectively. Adding 2 or more Durians may result in Monster Lasagna.

As it is not a Meat food item, Wigfrid will not eat it.

Prerequisites

  • Requires:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes:
  • Excludes: 
  • Excludes:

Recipe

×3

Examples

×2

50% chance to result in Fruit Medley

×3

×3

Usage

×4

Bugs

  • Fist Full of Jam does not exclude Fishes. Shipwrecked DLC introduces food items that have fish values without any meat value, such as Fish Morsel and Jellyfish. This causes using up to 2 Fish Morsels or using a single Jellyfish in a recipe with Fruits to produce Fist Full of Jam despite containing meat.

Tips

  • Making Fruit Medley is a more efficient use of Durian Fruit by adding a stick instead of a fourth fruit, getting much more healing, more sanity, and slightly more hunger return per fruit.
    • You can put 3 berries and a Durian fruit to get this item, which results in the same hunger and health bonus as just the berries (roasted), but slower rot time and 5 sanity added.
  • Eating the individual ingredients cooked is almost always better than using them to make Fist Full of Jam, unless the player needs the small Sanity boost or needs to keep the food longer. Jam keeps for 15 days, compared to Berries' 6 and Roasted Berries' meager 3.
    • Another possible reason to do this is to freshen a pile of Berries to be effective whilst using Wickerbottom. This works with spoiling Berries, meaning the player can gain Sanity and the full amount of Hunger

Gallery

  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.

–Willow

–Wendy
“THERE'S JAM IN MY CIRCUITRY”
–WX-78

–Wickerbottom
“Now to find peanut butter.”
–Woodie
“It's soiled my good gloves.”
–Maxwell
“I predict eating this will be an enjoyable experience.”
–Wagstaff
“Sticky and gröss.”
–Wigfrid
“Ew... sticky fingies.”
–Webber
“Preserve those jams.”
–Walani
“Simple, sweet, parfait.”
–Warly
“Prepared ta perfection.”
–Woodlegs
“JELLY FOR'ST WILBA BELLY”
–Wilba
“Sweet belly stuff”
–Wormwood
“No bread. I guess I'll just eat the whole thing straight out of the jar.”
–Wheeler
“The sweet taste of good planning.”
–Winona
“Oh no, it's in my fur.”
–Wortox
“Got claws all sticky.”
–Wurt
“Now we're really in a jam...”
–Walter
“A jar! That's what I forgot!”
–Wanda
Fist Full of Jam is a Fruit Food Item cooked in a Crock Pot by adding any Fruits except Dragon Fruit, which will lead to a Dragonpie instead. However, adding more than 2.5 units of Fruits may result in Fruit Medley instead. It takes 10 seconds to cook.
No Meats, Vegetables, or Twigs should be added, otherwise, it will result in Meatballs, Ratatouille, or Wet Goop respectively. Adding 2 or more Durians may result in Monster Lasagna.
As it is not a Meat food item, Wigfrid will not eat it.

Bugs[edit | edit source]
Fist Full of Jam does not exclude Fishes. Shipwrecked DLC introduces food items that have fish values without any meat value, such as Fish Morsel and Jellyfish. This causes using up to 2 Fish Morsels or using a single Jellyfish in a recipe with Fruits to produce Fist Full of Jam despite containing meat.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Making Fruit Medley is a more efficient use of Durian Fruit by adding a stick instead of a fourth fruit, getting much more healing, more sanity, and slightly more hunger return per fruit.
You can put 3 berries and a Durian fruit to get this item, which results in the same hunger and health bonus as just the berries (roasted), but slower rot time and 5 sanity added.
Eating the individual ingredients cooked is almost always better than using them to make Fist Full of Jam, unless the player needs the small Sanity boost or needs to keep the food longer. Jam keeps for 15 days, compared to Berries' 6 and Roasted Berries' meager 3.
Another possible reason to do this is to freshen a pile of Berries to be effective whilst using Wickerbottom. This works with spoiling Berries, meaning the player can gain Sanity and the full amount of Hunger

That's what I forgot! |choose = woodie }} '''Fist Full of Jam''' is a [[Fruit]] [[Food]] [[Items|Item]] cooked in a [[Crock Pot]] by adding any Fruits except [[Dragon Fruit]], which will lead to a [[Dragonpie]] instead. However, adding more than 2.5 units of Fruits may result in [[Fruit Medley]] instead. It takes 10 seconds to cook. No [[Meats]], [[Vegetables]], or [[Twigs]] should be added, otherwise, it will result in [[Meatballs]], [[Ratatouille]], or [[Wet Goop]] respectively. Adding 2 or more [[Durian|Durians]] may result in [[Monster Lasagna]]. As it is not a Meat food item, [[Wigfrid]] will not eat it. == Prerequisites == * Requires: [[File:Fruits Crock Pot(No Dragonfruit).png|link=Fruit]] * Excludes: [[File:Dragon Fruits.png|link=Dragon Fruit]] * Excludes: [[File:Inedible Crock Pot.png|link=Inedibles]] * Excludes: [[File:Meats Crock Pot.png|link=Meats]] * Excludes: [[File:Vegetables Crock Pot(No Mandrake).png|link=Vegetables]] == {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe == {{Recipe |item1 = Fruit |item2 = Filler |count2 = 3 |tool = Crock Pot |result = Fist Full of Jam }} == Examples == {{Recipe |item1 = Berries |item2 = Berries |item3 = Berries |item4 = Berries |tool = Crock Pot |result = Fist Full of Jam }} {{Recipe |item1 = Durians |item2 = Pomegranates |tool = Crock Pot |result = Fist Full of Jam |item3=Berry|count3=2|note=50% chance to result in [[Fruit Medley]]}} {{Recipe |item1 = Berry |item2 = Ice |count2 = 3 |tool = Crock Pot |result = Fist Full of Jam |dlc = Reign of Giants }} {{Recipe |item1 = Coffee Beans |count1 = 3 |item2 = Ice |tool = Crock Pot |result = Fist Full of Jam |dlc = shipwrecked }} {{Recipe |item1 = Berry |item2 = Banana SW |item3 = Cooked Fish Morsel |item4 = Fish Morsel |tool = Crock Pot |result = Fist Full of Jam |dlc = Shipwrecked }} == {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |item = Fist Full of Jam |tool = Pig Icon |result = Manure }} {{Recipe |item1 = Fist Full of Jam |tool = Pig Usher |result = Oinc |resultcount = 4 |dlc = Hamlet }} == {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * Fist Full of Jam does not exclude [[Fishes]]. ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC introduces food items that have fish values without any meat value, such as [[Fish Morsel]] and [[Jellyfish]]. This causes using up to 2 Fish Morsels or using a single Jellyfish in a recipe with Fruits to produce Fist Full of Jam despite containing meat. == {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips == * Making Fruit Medley is a more efficient use of Durian Fruit by adding a stick instead of a fourth fruit, getting much more healing, more sanity, and slightly more hunger return per fruit. **You can put 3 berries and a Durian fruit to get this item, which results in the same hunger and health bonus as just the berries (roasted), but slower rot time and 5 sanity added. *Eating the individual ingredients [[Cooking|cooked]] is almost always better than using them to make Fist Full of Jam, unless the player needs the small [[Sanity]] boost or needs to keep the food longer. Jam keeps for 15 days, compared to Berries' 6 and Roasted Berries' meager 3. **Another possible reason to do this is to freshen a pile of Berries to be effective whilst using [[Wickerbottom]]. This works with spoiling Berries, meaning the player can gain Sanity and the full amount of Hunger
